Committed and pushed workspace: PR triage results, schedule updates

queue.task: Plan JIT work: pre-loop codegen infrastructure
queue.summary: Planned pre-loop codegen: two-phase codegen with preLoopRegion flag in _emitGuardExit. Pre-loop guards emit simple return (no continue loop, no side-trace dispatch). LICM pass will conditionally allow guard hoisting when operands are loop-invariant. Key steps: preLoopRegion flag, simplified pre-loop guard exits, selective guard hoisting in LICM, dependent load/unbox chain hoisting.

queue.task: JIT: pre-loop codegen infrastructure (enables LICM guard hoisting)
queue.summary: Implemented pre-loop codegen infrastructure: (1) Added _inLoop flag to TraceCompiler, (2) LOOP_START now emits loop label during IR iteration instead of before, (3) _emitGuardExit uses simple returns (no side-trace dispatch/continue loop) when pre-loop, (4) LICM pass now allows hoisting GUARD_INT/BOOL/STRING/ARRAY/HASH/BOUNDS/TRUTHY/FALSY when operands are loop-invariant. Verified: hash benchmark hoists 9 instructions including 3 GUARD_HASH guards. 234 tests pass, no benchmark regressions.
Verified pre-loop codegen impact: array benchmarks jumped from 0.96x/1.56x to 10.7x/10.9x. Aggregate 8.57x→9.56x. 234 tests pass. Pre-loop infrastructure from T8 working correctly.
Pre-loop codegen complete as of T8/T9. Optimization pipeline verified: 10 passes, all working. Array benchmarks confirmed 10x+. No further pre-loop work needed.

queue.task: Commit, run full test suite, benchmarks
queue.summary: Git committed+pushed workspace. Dashboard healthy. 234 tests pass. No actionable GH notifications. 1 mention on telegram voice bug (not actionable).
Removed 7 redundant LICM/pre-loop slots (T11-T12, T15-T19). Pre-loop codegen fully done. Plan: V2 dashboard integration next, deopt for Session B. 5hr runway to boundary.

queue.task: JIT: complete pre-loop codegen + fix LICM
queue.skip_reason: LICM already complete and verified in T8-T10
Just completed MAINTAIN in T13, no new changes to commit

queue.task: Assess JIT codegen quality, plan next optimization
queue.summary: JIT at 9.56x aggregate. Removed 5 redundant dashboard slots (already integrated). Added PLAN+5 BUILD for deoptimization as next major JIT milestone. Side traces (2-3x) and hash lookups (3x) are weakest benchmarks.

queue.goal: Plan deoptimization: snapshot capture at guards, state reconstruction on exit
queue.summary: Planned 5 deopt BUILD tasks: snapshot capture in recording, codegen emit, VM resume at exact PC, optimizer snapshot maintenance, tests. Key insight: JS-targeted snapshots are just object literals mapping slots to variable names.

queue.task: Snapshot capture: add snapshot field to guard IR instructions during recording
queue.summary: Snapshot capture implemented: all guard instructions now have .snapshot field with locals/globals→IR ref maps. TraceRecorder tracks slot state, addGuardInst auto-captures. 234 tests pass, no benchmark regression (9.4x).

queue.task: Codegen: emit snapshot data in guard exit returns (map IR refs to JS vars)
queue.summary: Codegen emits snapshot data in guard exit returns. Promoted globals/locals use __cachedInteger(vN) for boxing. Non-promoted use direct varName. 234 tests pass, 9.35x aggregate (no regression).

queue.task: VM: use snapshot to resume interpreter at exact bytecode position on guard exit
queue.summary: VM snapshot restoration on guard exit

queue.task: Optimizer: update snapshots when CSE/LICM/DCE modify IR refs
queue.summary: Optimizer snapshot maintenance: _replaceRef, _compact, LICM, DCE all update snapshot refs. 234 tests pass.

queue.task: Deopt tests: verify correct snapshot restoration, no regressions
queue.summary: 10 deopt tests added: snapshot capture (3), codegen emission (2), correctness (5). All verify snapshots survive optimization, appear in compiled code, and produce correct results. 244 total tests pass.

queue.task: Commit, PR check, daily log update

queue.summary: Pivoting to EXPLORE: sea-of-nodes IR deep dive. Most interesting research direction — used by V8 TurboFan, Graal, HHVM. Could inform future optimizer architecture.

Sea-of-nodes deep dive. Key insight: linear IR is correct for trace JIT. SoN shines for method compilers with arbitrary control flow. Steal: explicit dependency edges for easier LICM, late scheduling heuristic. Full SoN would over-engineer traces.

queue.skip_reason: SoN exploration complete, key insight captured

queue.summary: Synthesized: linear IR correct for traces, deopt infrastructure enables more speculation, next explore V8 deopt details for lazy deopt + OSR.

queue.task: Deoptimization deep dive: V8 deopt, OSR
queue.summary: V8 deopt + OSR deep dive. Key findings: (1) our trace JIT already does implicit OSR entry/exit, (2) lazy deopt not needed for Monkey, (3) deopt loop prevention already handled by side traces. Global invalidation via version counters identified as future improvement.

queue.skip_reason: Too early for day review, 4.5h left

queue.skip_reason: Pivoting to BUILD — side trace optimization more impactful

queue.summary: README updated: 9.5x, 244 tests, 10 passes, deopt snapshots, side trace inlining, blog link added. Pushed.
Skipped: replaced by T85
MAINTAIN: dashboard queue synced, all committed and pushed. Blog live. 5 tasks done this session.

queue.task: Review blog publish, assess JIT direction
queue.summary: THINK: Blog published successfully. JIT direction: allocation sinking is the highest-impact next optimization (eliminates object allocation in hot loops). Instruction scheduling lower priority since V8 handles it. Focus next cycle on allocation sinking.

PLAN: Allocation sinking — 4 BUILD tasks: (1) track virtual BOX_INT, delay materialization, (2) extend to guard exits with deopt snapshots, (3) virtual arrays/hashes, (4) tests+benchmarks. Linear trace = no merge points = simpler than Graal PEA.

queue.task: Yield: Allocation sinking has minimal impact — variable promotion already eliminates most BOX_INT allocations. Remaining allocations escape to arrays/snapshots. Need to reassess optimization targets.
queue.summary: THINK: Reassessed targets. Hash lookups (2.3x) are weakest — bottleneck is Map.get(fastHashKey()) every iteration with constant keys. Two approaches: (1) hoist fastHashKey() computation before loop, (2) hash inline caching with version checks. Also: free variable access for closures. Pivoting to hash optimization.

queue.goal: Allocation sinking has minimal impact — variable promotion already eliminates most BOX_INT allocations. Remaining allocations escape to arrays/snapshots. Need to reassess optimization targets.
queue.summary: PLAN: Pivoted from allocation sinking to hash optimization. 3 BUILD tasks: (1) hoist fastHashKey() + cache pair lookups, (2) hash inline cache, (3) tests+benchmarks. Target: hash 2.3x → 4x+.

queue.task: Hash optimization: hoist fastHashKey() for constant keys + cache hash pair lookups for loop-invariant hashes
Hash optimization: add hash inline cache for repeated lookups on same object
Benchmark verification + test for hash optimizations
Allocation sinking pass: track virtual BOX_INT through IR, delay materialization until escape
queue.summary: YIELD: Investigated allocation sinking. Variable promotion already eliminates most BOX_INT in hot loops. Remaining allocations (array push, write-back, snapshots) all escape — sinking wont help. Need to reassess: hash inline caching or loop unrolling more impactful.

queue.task: Extend sinking to BOX_INT in guard exits (deopt snapshots reconstruct from raw values)
Extend sinking to array/hash allocation (virtual arrays that don't escape)
Tests + benchmarks for allocation sinking (measure allocation reduction)
Assess allocation sinking, plan instruction scheduling
Allocation sinking deep dive: Graal partial escape analysis
Free curiosity: follow most interesting thread
Final Session B commits, push all repos
Optimize side trace dispatch: reduce guard exit overhead
queue.summary: Switched sideTraces from Map to plain Object for property access. Marginal improvement (~0.1x). Real bottleneck is wb/reload round-trip on side trace calls. Needs trace recompilation with inlined side trace body for major improvement. 244 tests pass.

queue.task: Side trace inlining: recompile parent with inlined side trace body for loop_back exits
queue.summary: Side trace inlining: parent recompiled with inlined side trace body. Eliminates wb/reload round-trip. 7.1x for 100k-iteration branching (vs 3.2x at 10k). 244 tests pass.

queue.task: Write blog post: Building a Tracing JIT in JavaScript
queue.summary: Blog post draft: Building a Tracing JIT in JavaScript (~9KB). Covers: architecture, recording, IR, 10 optimization passes, variable promotion, side traces, deoptimization snapshots, results (9.5x). Ready for review/publishing.
